body: Phish is a jam band, meaning their songs can last anywhere between 4–30 minutes. Our challenge was to develop a real-time creative ecosystem that allowed us to follow along with the band live during their performance. Ensuring we had individual content for 86 songs spanned across 4 nights that could live for 30+ minutes was essential.
This amount of content created over 400 terabytes of data that had to be transferred from 4 different countries all the way to our onsite servers at the Sphere in Las Vegas.

I was brought on to lead the brainstorm and ideation of creative, production and delivery of content, lead all Unreal development and implementation, oversee all onsite technical innovation and capabilities, and ensure a proper and safe delivery of the show. I led a team that developed visuals that merge with scenographic elements, 360° live performance capture, and emerging real-time technology including Unreal and Notch, as well as pre-rendered imagery and AI-generated visuals.

We partnered with three different agencies (Sila Sveta, Fly Studios, Myreze), as well as internal and external artists, to develop 12+ hours of pre-rendered, real-time, and AI-generated content. We utilized Disguise's asset management platform and a GrandMA2 console to control and layer all of the content in real time — pre-programming hundreds of looks that could be seamlessly layered, transitioned, and executed live while the band was playing.
led: Led creative ideation, production, and content delivery
Led all Unreal development and implementation
Oversaw onsite technical innovation and safe show delivery

impact.note: The show was a tremendous success — 4 sold-out nights at the Sphere, 80,000 fans live, and clips viewed over 20,000,000 times across social.
